raw: {"weights":{"R1.1":0.7,"R1.2":1.0,"R1.3":1.0,"R1.4":0.3,"R1.5":0.0,"R1.6":0.3,"R1.7":0.3,"RX.1":1.0,"RX.2":1.0,"RX.3":1.0,"RX.4":1.0,"RX.5":1.0},"active_set":["R1.1","R1.2","R1.3","RX.1","RX.2","RX.3","RX.4","RX.5"],"rationale":{"R1.1":"Section presents a thesis (SpectralPINN surrogate), a gap statement (mesh-solver multi-scale limitations), and a contribution claim (sinusoidal-activation PINN); coherence and accuracy of these framing elements are expected in any section review.","R1.2":"Multiple logical chains require verification: the described training objective (MSE against DNS snapshots only) appears inconsistent with the PINN label, which implies a physics-residual loss; the concluding claim that results 'demonstrate embedding physical priors' does not follow if no PDE constraint is enforced during training—this is a load-bearing internal contradiction.","R1.3":"Section asserts specific quantitative results (4.2%/6.7% L2 error, 40× speedup, 9.1% ablation velocity error) and causal claims ('activation choice is the principal driver of accuracy'); these must be tightly grounded in reported experimental evidence, and the absence of uncertainty bounds or significance testing weakens alignment."},"clarification_needed":true,"clarification_question":"Should this review prioritize technical and scientific validity (logical consistency, evidence-claim alignment, PINN framing accuracy) or writing and editorial quality (prose clarity, paragraph structure, terminology precision)?"}

rationale.R1.1: Section presents a thesis (SpectralPINN surrogate), a gap statement (mesh-solver multi-scale limitations), and a contribution claim (sinusoidal-activation PINN); coherence and accuracy of these framing elements are expected in any section review.
rationale.R1.2: Multiple logical chains require verification: the described training objective (MSE against DNS snapshots only) appears inconsistent with the PINN label, which implies a physics-residual loss; the concluding claim that results 'demonstrate embedding physical priors' does not follow if no PDE constraint is enforced during training—this is a load-bearing internal contradiction.
rationale.R1.3: Section asserts specific quantitative results (4.2%/6.7% L2 error, 40× speedup, 9.1% ablation velocity error) and causal claims ('activation choice is the principal driver of accuracy'); these must be tightly grounded in reported experimental evidence, and the absence of uncertainty bounds or significance testing weakens alignment.

parsed.clarification_question: Should this review prioritize technical and scientific validity (logical consistency, evidence-claim alignment, PINN framing accuracy) or writing and editorial quality (prose clarity, paragraph structure, terminology precision)?
